metaDescription: Understand Zapier's free plan task limits, single-step restrictions, and update intervals so your automations don't pause unexpectedly.
intro: It's easy to hit an invisible wall on Zapier's free plan without realizing why your automations stopped working. This guide breaks down the free tier's core limits, from the monthly task cap to single-step Zap restrictions and app availability, so you can plan around them or decide when it's time to upgrade.

steps.heading: Know your monthly task allotment
steps.body: The Free Plan caps usage at 100 tasks per month, and every successful automation step counts toward that limit.

steps.heading: Understand single-step restrictions
steps.body: Free accounts are limited to one trigger and one action per Zap, so multi-step workflows require an upgrade.

steps.heading: Account for the update interval
steps.body: Free Zaps check for new data on a 15-minute polling interval, which affects how real-time your automations feel.

steps.heading: Check which apps are locked
steps.body: Some integrations, like Salesforce, Shopify, or Webhooks by Zapier, are restricted to paid tiers.

steps.heading: Track your usage in the dashboard
steps.body: Review the task history dashboard to see how successful operations are counted, noting that filtered or errored steps aren't included.

steps.heading: Use workarounds to stay under the limit
steps.body: Apply built-in application rules before sending data to Zapier to reduce unnecessary task usage.

faq.question: How many tasks does the Zapier free plan allow per month?
faq.answer: The Free Plan is limited to 100 tasks per month.

faq.question: Can I build multi-step Zaps for free?
faq.answer: No, the free plan restricts you to a single trigger and a single action per Zap.

faq.question: Why does my Zap take a few minutes to trigger?
faq.answer: Free plan Zaps check for new data on a 15-minute polling interval rather than triggering instantly.

faq.question: What happens when I hit my task limit?
faq.answer: Automation runs pause once you exceed your monthly task limit until the cycle resets or you upgrade your plan.
